description_long: This sculpture depicts a reclining draped female figure, carved in white marble with intricate attention to the folds of her garment. The figure rests on her left side, propped up by her left arm, with her right arm bent and hand raised to her head, possibly shielding her eyes or expressing a gesture of contemplation. The drapery is rendered with deep, flowing lines, creating a sense of movement and softness in contrast to the hard stone. The base is rectangular and appears to be made of a different, slightly darker stone, providing a stable platform for the figure. The sculpture shows signs of age and some surface wear, with portions of the head and extremities missing or eroded. The overall impression is classical and serene, reminiscent of ancient Greco-Roman funerary or mythological art.
description: A reclining draped female figure in white marble, resting on a rectangular stone base with detailed, flowing garment folds and a contemplative pose.
description_view_0: The front-left view highlights the figure's face, bent right arm, and the intricate drapery over her body, with the left arm supporting her head.
description_view_1: The side view shows the narrow profile of the sculpture, emphasizing the depth of the drapery and the thickness of the base.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the back of the figure and the full length of the draped garment, with the head and arm positions less visible.
description_view_3: The right side view displays the figure's bent knees, the flow of the garment, and the supporting base, with the head and upper body partially obscured.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
